name: pre-commit

metadata.name: pre-commit
metadata.summary: A framework for managing and maintaining multi-language pre-commit hooks.

documentation:
[![build status](https://github.com/pre-commit/pre-commit/actions/workflows/main.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/pre-commit/pre-commit/actions/workflows/main.yml)
[![pre-commit.ci status](https://results.pre-commit.ci/badge/github/pre-commit/pre-commit/main.svg)](https://results.pre-commit.ci/latest/github/pre-commit/pre-commit/main)

## pre-commit

A framework for managing and maintaining multi-language pre-commit hooks.

For more information see: https://pre-commit.com/

functions.name: pre_commit.color.format_color
documentation:
Format text with color.

Args:
    text - Text to be formatted with color if `use_color`
    color - The color start string
    use_color_setting - Whether or not to color

functions.name: pre_commit.color.use_color
documentation:
Choose whether to use color based on the command argument.

Args:
    setting - Either `auto`, `always`, or `never`

functions.name: pre_commit.envcontext.envcontext
documentation:
In this context, `os.environ` is modified according to `patch`.

`patch` is an iterable of 2-tuples (key, value):
    `key`: string
    `value`:
        - string: `environ[key] == value` inside the context.
        - UNSET: `key not in environ` inside the context.
        - template: A template is a tuple of strings and Var which will be
          replaced with the previous environment

functions.name: pre_commit.commands.autoupdate._original_lines
documentation: detect `rev:` lines or reformat the file

functions.name: pre_commit.commands.autoupdate.autoupdate
documentation: Auto-update the pre-commit config to the latest versions of repos.

functions.name: pre_commit.clientlib.parse_version
documentation: poor man's version comparison

functions.name: pre_commit.clientlib._entry
documentation:
the hook `entry` is passed through `shlex.split()` by the command
runner, so to prevent issues with spaces and backslashes (on Windows)
it must be quoted here.

functions.name: pre_commit.commands.run._compute_cols
documentation:
Compute the number of columns to display hook messages.  The widest
that will be displayed is in the no files skipped case:

    Hook name...(no files to check) Skipped

functions.name: pre_commit.commands.run._run_hooks
documentation: Actually run the hooks.

functions.name: pre_commit.git.check_for_cygwin_mismatch
documentation: See https://github.com/pre-commit/pre-commit/issues/354

functions.name: pre_commit.git.get_best_candidate_tag
documentation:
Get the best tag candidate.

Multiple tags can exist on a SHA. Sometimes a moving tag is attached
to a version tag. Try to pick the tag that looks like a version.

variables.name: pre_commit.languages.docker.in_env
documentation: no special environment for docker

functions.name: pre_commit.languages.lua._get_lua_version
documentation: Get the Lua version used in file paths.

functions.name: pre_commit.languages.rust._rust_toolchain
documentation: Transform the language version into a rust toolchain version.

functions.name: pre_commit.languages.python.bin_dir
documentation: On windows there's a different directory for the virtualenv

functions.name: pre_commit.lang_base._shuffled
documentation: Deterministically shuffle

methods.name: pre_commit.lang_base.Language.ENVIRONMENT_DIR
documentation: Use `None` for no installation / environment

methods.name: pre_commit.lang_base.Language.get_default_version
documentation: return a value to replace `'default` for `language_version`

methods.name: pre_commit.lang_base.Language.health_check
documentation: return whether the environment is healthy (or should be rebuilt)

methods.name: pre_commit.lang_base.Language.install_environment
documentation: install a repository for the given language and language_version

methods.name: pre_commit.lang_base.Language.in_env
documentation: modify the environment for hook execution

methods.name: pre_commit.lang_base.Language.run_hook
documentation: execute a hook and return the exit code and output

functions.name: pre_commit.parse_shebang.normalize_cmd
documentation:
Fixes for the following issues on windows
- https://bugs.python.org/issue8557
- windows does not parse shebangs

This function also makes deep-path shebangs work just fine

functions.name: pre_commit.staged_files_only.staged_files_only
documentation:
Clear any unstaged changes from the git working directory inside this
context.

functions.name: pre_commit.languages.r._r_code_in_tempfile
documentation:
To avoid quoting and escaping issues, avoid `Rscript [options] -e {expr}`
but use `Rscript [options] path/to/file_with_expr.R`

functions.name: pre_commit.languages.r._entry_validate
documentation:
Allowed entries:
# Rscript -e expr
# Rscript path/to/file

functions.name: pre_commit.languages.r._inline_r_setup
documentation:
Some behaviour of R cannot be configured via env variables, but can
only be configured via R options once R has started. These are set here.

functions.name: pre_commit.xargs.xargs
documentation:
A simplified implementation of xargs.

color: Make a pty if on a platform that supports it
target_concurrency: Target number of partitions to run concurrently

functions.name: pre_commit.store._get_default_directory
documentation:
Returns the default directory for the Store.  This is intentionally
underscored to indicate that `Store.get_default_directory` is the intended
way to get this information.  This is also done so
`Store.get_default_directory` can be mocked in tests and
`_get_default_directory` can be tested.

methods.name: pre_commit.store.Store._complete_clone
documentation: Perform a complete clone of a repository and its submodules

methods.name: pre_commit.store.Store._shallow_clone
documentation: Perform a shallow clone of a repository and its submodules

methods.name: pre_commit.store.Store.clone
documentation: Clone the given url and checkout the specific ref.

functions.name: pre_commit.util.clean_path_on_failure
documentation: Cleans up the directory on an exceptional failure.

functions.name: pre_commit.util.rmtree
documentation: On windows, rmtree fails for readonly dirs.
